preamble: The transformation of the voiced alveolar fricative [z] into its voiceless counterpart [s] is a common phonetic shift occurring through final-devoicing or assimilation to adjacent voiceless sounds.

examples.note: Standard final-devoicing where voiced sibilants in word-final positions are realized as voiceless.
Widespread merger of voiced and voiceless sibilants during the medieval period (seseo/merger).
